summary: Ukrainian strike drones have repeatedly hit Russian fuel infrastructure, including a refinery 15 km from the Kremlin and facilities in Kapotnya, prompting heightened Russian air defenses. Kyiv ordered a surge of FPV kamikaze drones targeting civilian sites in Zaporizhzhia, while Russian forces continue to strike Ukrainian refineries in Poltava and Boryspol. Both sides report increased drone activity across multiple fronts.
key_points: Ukrainian drones struck a Moscow refinery in Kapotnya, located 15 km from the Kremlin, damaging a key fuel asset (Source: @UkraineNow/82010).
Russia reported multiple hits on its Moscow refinery, indicating sustained aerial attacks (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67897).
Kyiv ordered an increase in FPV kamikaze drone deliveries to Russian units for attacks on civilian infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia (Source: @UkraineNow/82027).
Russian air defenses downed several drones over Moscow, with collateral damage to a local shopping centre (Source: @UkraineNow/82012).
Russian forces struck the Zaturovo oil refinery in Poltava and a fuel storage site in Boryspol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30995).
Multiple Ukrainian strike drones were observed over northern and eastern regions, including Chernihiv, Sumy, Kharkiv, and near Kyiv (Source: @kpszsu/65526).
Russia plans to import gasoline by sea to offset fuel shortages after Ukrainian attacks on its refineries (Source: @UkraineNow/82008).
A Shahed drone was confirmed over Pavlograd, heading south‑west (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67865).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian drones have hit a Moscow refinery only 15 km from the Kremlin, prompting intensified Russian air defenses. Kyiv has ordered a surge of FPV kamikaze drones for attacks on civilian targets in Zaporizhzhia. Russian forces continue striking Ukrainian fuel facilities in Poltava and Boryspol.

points: Ukrainian strike drones were reported over the Chernihiv region moving south, over Sumy moving west, and over Kharkiv moving south‑west, indicating broad operational reach across the north‑east (Source: @kpszsu/65526).
A drone attack was observed heading toward Dobroslava from Mykolaiv region, showing Ukrainian UAVs operating from the south‑west (Source: @kpszsu/65509).
Ukrainian forces directed a strike drone toward the area of Ochakiv from the Black Sea, highlighting maritime‑origin UAV operations (Source: @kpszsu/65508).
An attack drone was sighted south of Sumy heading west, suggesting continued UAV pressure on northern Sumy oblast (Source: @kpszsu/65516).
A UAV was reported in Shostka district of Sumy moving west, adding to the pattern of northern UAV activity (Source: @kpszsu/65534).
Russian officials reported multiple hits on the Moscow oil refinery in Kapotnya, a strategic fuel hub for the capital (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67897).
Russia announced plans to import gasoline by sea to mitigate fuel shortages caused by Ukrainian attacks on its refineries (Source: @UkraineNow/82008).
Russian air defence units successfully intercepted several Ukrainian drones over Moscow, though a local shopping centre sustained damage (Source: @UkraineNow/82012).
Russian forces struck the Zaturovo oil refinery in Poltava region and a fuel storage facility in Boryspol, targeting Ukrainian energy logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30995).
A Shahed‑type loitering munition was confirmed over Pavlograd, heading south‑west, indicating Russian‑supplied UAV activity in the eastern theater (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67865).
Ukrainian strike drones were observed over the Chernihiv region moving south, suggesting pressure on Russian‑held northern positions (Source: @kpszsu/65526).
Ukrainian UAVs operating from the Black Sea towards Ochakiv demonstrate maritime‑linked strike capability on the southern front (Source: @kpszsu/65508).
Drone activity reported near Barishivka, flying toward Kyiv, reflects ongoing aerial threats to the capital corridor (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67863).
Ukrainian drones struck a Moscow refinery in Kapotnya, only 15 km from the Kremlin, delivering a significant blow to Russian fuel infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82010).
Russian air defence downed several drones over Moscow, with collateral damage to a local mall, showing heightened defensive posture (Source: @UkraineNow/82012).
A Shahed drone was observed near Brovary, changing course toward the Dniprovsky district of Kyiv, indicating potential threat to the capital's outskirts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67882).
Multiple UAV sightings over northern oblasts, including Sumy and Chernihiv, demonstrate expanded Ukrainian aerial reach (Source: @kpszsu/65526).
Kyiv ordered an increase in FPV kamikaze drone deliveries to Russian units for intensified attacks on civilian infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia (Source: @UkraineNow/82027).
Ukrainian drones were reported heading toward Dobroslava from Mykolaiv region, showing south‑west to south‑east operational lines (Source: @kpszsu/65509).
A strike drone was observed south of Sumy moving west, indicating pressure on Russian positions in the northern part of the southern front (Source: @kpszsu/65516).
Ukrainian UAV activity over the Black Sea towards Ochakiv suggests maritime‑linked strike capability affecting southern logistics (Source: @kpszsu/65508).
Ukrainian drones successfully hit the Moscow Kapotnya refinery, a high‑value target within 15 km of the Kremlin, demonstrating deep‑strike capability (Source: @UkraineNow/82010).
Russian air defence intercepted several drones over Moscow, though a shopping centre was damaged, highlighting both defensive success and collateral risk (Source: @UkraineNow/82012).

Multiple Shahed‑type loitering munitions were reported over Pavlograd, Barishivka, and near Brovary, indicating continued use of Russian‑supplied UAVs on the ground (Sources: @mon1tor_ua/67865, @mon1tor_ua/67863, @mon1tor_ua/67882).
Ukrainian strike drones were observed over northern oblasts moving south, showing a pattern of aerial pressure across the front lines (Source: @kpszsu/65526).

key_developments: Ukrainian drones struck the Moscow Kapotnya refinery, located 15 km from the Kremlin, delivering a strategic blow to Russian fuel supplies (Source: @UkraineNow/82010).
Kyiv ordered a surge of FPV kamikaze drones for attacks on civilian infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia, reflecting an escalation in UAV‑based terror tactics (Source: @UkraineNow/82027).
Russian air defence downed several drones over Moscow, with a local mall sustaining damage, indicating intensified aerial engagements over the capital (Source: @UkraineNow/82012).
Russian forces hit the Zaturovo oil refinery in Poltava and a fuel storage site in Boryspol, targeting Ukrainian energy logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30995).
A Shahed drone was confirmed over Pavlograd, heading south‑west, showing continued Russian‑supplied UAV activity on the eastern front (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67865).
Multiple Ukrainian strike drones were observed over Chernihiv, Sumy, and Kharkiv regions, moving south or west, indicating broad UAV operational reach (Source: @kpszsu/65526).
Russia plans to import gasoline by sea to offset shortages after Ukrainian attacks on its refineries, highlighting logistical strain (Source: @UkraineNow/82008).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV operations have demonstrated the ability to strike deep into Russian territory, notably the Kapotnya refinery near Moscow, challenging Russian air defence and critical infrastructure. Russia's reliance on imported gasoline underscores vulnerability in its fuel logistics following repeated Ukrainian attacks on refineries. The proliferation of Shahed loitering munitions on the eastern front continues to pose a persistent threat to Ukrainian ground forces and civilian areas.
sections.regional_response: Russian authorities have intensified air defence measures over Moscow, successfully intercepting several drones but still suffering infrastructure damage. Kyiv's directive to increase FPV kamikaze drone deliveries reflects a strategic shift toward targeting civilian morale in occupied regions. International partners, such as Germany and the UK, are advancing air defence cooperation, though immediate operational impacts remain limited.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of UAV flight paths is essential, as strike drones are operating from maritime origins and across multiple oblasts, suggesting flexible launch points. The emergence of Shahed drones over key eastern locales indicates sustained supply lines for Russian‑provided UAVs. Russian fuel import plans may create maritime interdiction opportunities for Ukrainian naval forces.
